title: How I Scaled $30K to $170K Using a Repeatable Strategy
author: Zealousideal-Hat4801
selftext:
I didn’t come from a finance background   I actually started out as a lumberjack.

Spending long days in the woods taught me a different kind of discipline: patience, consistency, and respecting risk. You don’t rush the process, and you definitely don’t ignore warning signs  because mistakes have consequences.  
  
Over time, my investment portfolio grew from approximately $30,000 to around $170,000.

What surprised me most was not the returns themselves, but rather the enhanced stability of the portfolio once I had simplified everything.

In the past, I often overcomplicated matters  constantly switching strategies and searching for better approaches. In reality, however, this did more harm than good, ultimately hindering my execution.

My current approach is remarkably simple: I begin with a higher time frame (the 4-hour chart) to identify the trading range, wait for sufficient liquidity to build, and execute a trade only after confirming that the market structure is sound.

The real differentiator lies in consistently adhering to a strategy that prioritizes selectively reducing\* the number of trades, rather than increasing them.

While this may not be a revolutionary innovation, organizing all these elements into a repeatable framework has proven immensely valuable to me.

Over time, I have continued to refine and more clearly document my methodology  primarily to ensure consistency in my strategy.

Over time, the key lies in repeatedly executing the correct processes. I have compiled my specific configuration settings into a folder and am sharing them completely free of charge with anyone who needs them. Creating this configuration was no easy feat, so let's keep up the hard work!
